description: Payloads for stored/persistent XSS. Focus: high-impact execution contexts (admin panels, rich editors, comment fields).
approach: 1. Inject payload in user-supplied content (comment, bio, product name, message)
3. When admin/moderator views the content, payload executes in THEIR context
4. Impact: steal admin session cookie, perform admin actions, add backdoor admin account

admin_panel_targets: Monitor: report/export pages that render user data
session_hijack_chain: Step 1: Inject payload with cookie exfiltration
Step 2: Admin views page, payload executes
Step 3: Admin cookie sent to OOB callback
Step 4: Use stolen cookie to authenticate as admin
Step 5: Create backdoor admin account or exfiltrate data

impact_escalation.from_reflected_to_stored: If reflected XSS found, look for stored variant: does the parameter get saved anywhere (profile, preferences, log)?
impact_escalation.from_self_xss_to_stored: Self-XSS can be elevated via CSRF: force victim to submit XSS payload to their own account, then it's stored and visible to admins
